description: Fix TOCTOU race condition in plugin SDK DOM utilities (waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval) where elements added between the initial querySelector check and the MutationObserver.observe() call are missed, causing promises to hang until timeout.

userStories.title: Fix waitForSelector/waitForSelectorRemoval race between initial check and observer setup
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval to reliably detect elements that appear (or disappear) between the initial querySelector check and the MutationObserver.observe() call, so that my tool handlers do not hang until timeout when the DOM changes during that window.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForSelector re-queries document.querySelector immediately after calling observer.observe(), resolving if the element was added between the initial check and the observe call
waitForSelectorRemoval re-queries document.querySelector immediately after calling observer.observe(), resolving if the element was removed between the initial check and the observe call
The re-query is guarded by the same `settled` flag to prevent double-resolve if the observer callback fires simultaneously
Existing tests continue to pass unchanged

New test: waitForSelector resolves when element is added synchronously between initial check and observer setup (simulated by adding element before the observer's first callback fires, using a mock that returns null on first call and an element on second call)
New test: waitForSelectorRemoval resolves when element is removed synchronously between initial check and observer setup
